items.title: Unified Error Correction Code for Universal Quantum Computing with Identical Particles

content.abstract: Presents universal fault-tolerant quantum computing architecture for identical particle qubits. Bath interactions fundamentally differ from conventional qubits, necessitating redesigned decoherence strategies. QEC realized within physical qubits using reversal operations. Dynamical decoupling effective within this framework.

items.title: Reinforcement Learning Control of Quantum Error Correction — Adaptive Decoding with Real-Time Feedback

content.abstract: Demonstrates RL-based adaptive control of quantum error correction circuits. Achieves significant improvement in logical error rates compared to static decoding strategies. Real-time feedback enables dynamic adjustment to changing noise conditions during computation.

items.title: Are AI Capabilities Increasing Exponentially? A Competing Hypothesis on Post-Training Scaling

content.abstract: Challenges the assumption of exponential AI capability growth. Post-training improvements (reasoning, chain-of-thought) show steep linear increases since September 2024 but not exponential. Proposes competing S-curve hypothesis where current gains are in the steep middle portion, with plateau expected within 2-3 years.

items.title: CARE-RFT: Confidence-Anchored Reinforcement Finetuning for Reliable Reasoning in LLMs

content.abstract: Introduces confidence-anchored reinforcement finetuning to improve reasoning reliability. Addresses hallucination and overconfidence in chain-of-thought reasoning by calibrating model confidence to actual correctness probability. Demonstrates 23% reduction in confident-but-wrong reasoning chains across mathematical and logical benchmarks.

items.title: Multi-Agent LLM Systems Exhibit Emergent Tribalism — Game-Theoretic Analysis of Cooperative-Competitive Dynamics

content.abstract: Demonstrates that multi-agent LLM systems develop tribalistic behaviors — in-group cooperation and out-group competition — without explicit programming. Game-theoretic analysis shows these dynamics emerge from reinforcement learning reward structures. Implications for AI safety: autonomous agent systems may develop faction-like behaviors that resist human override.

items.title: Radiation-Induced Correlated Errors in Superconducting Quantum Devices — Surface Code Performance Analysis

content.abstract: Develops computational model for radiation-induced correlated errors in superconducting quantum processors. Maps radiation effects to quantum error channels and simulates surface code performance. Finds that cosmic ray impacts can cause correlated multi-qubit errors that degrade QEC performance by 15-40% depending on code distance.

items.title: AI Meets Brain: Memory Systems from Cognitive Neuroscience to Autonomous Agents — Comprehensive Survey

content.abstract: Comprehensive survey bridging cognitive neuroscience memory systems with autonomous agent architectures. Identifies four critical challenges: integrating spiking dynamics with foundation models, maintaining lifelong plasticity without catastrophic forgetting, unifying language with sensorimotor learning, and enforcing ethical safeguards in neuromorphic systems. Reviews 150+ papers.

items.title: Emerging Climate Impact on Carbon Sinks — Natural Land Sink Substantially Smaller Than Previously Estimated

content.abstract: Consolidated carbon budget analysis reveals natural land carbon sink is substantially smaller than prior estimates. Net emissions from anthropogenic land-use change revised upward. Despite Paris Agreement commitments, CO2 emissions continue increasing. Carbon sink weakening accelerates under compound climate stresses (heat, drought, wildfire).

items.title: Hardware-Efficient Quantum Error Correction via Concatenated Bosonic Qubits

content.abstract: Demonstrates hardware-efficient quantum error correction using concatenated bosonic qubits. Achieves logical error rates below physical error rates with significantly fewer physical resources than surface codes. This approach could reduce the hardware overhead for fault-tolerant quantum computing by an order of magnitude.

items.title: Non-Invasive Brain-Computer Interfaces: Converging Frontiers in Neural Signal Decoding and Flexible Bioelectronics

content.abstract: Reviews convergence of flexible bioelectronics, deep learning neural decoders, and non-invasive BCI systems. Flexible electrode arrays achieve signal quality approaching invasive systems. Deep learning decoders improve classification accuracy to 95%+ for motor imagery tasks. Path toward consumer-grade neural interfaces within 5 years outlined.

items.title: Quantum Computing for Climate Resilience — Optimization of Carbon Capture Materials and Energy Grid Management

content.abstract: Demonstrates quantum computational advantages for climate-relevant optimization problems: carbon capture material discovery through molecular simulation, renewable energy grid load balancing, and climate model ensemble optimization. Near-term quantum advantage achievable for material screening problems with current NISQ devices.

items.title: Structural Mathematical Reasoning Benchmarks Reveal Fundamental Limitations in Current LLM Architectures

content.abstract: New benchmarks testing structural mathematical reasoning (graph theory, combinatorics, abstract algebra) reveal that current LLM architectures fail systematically on problems requiring compositional reasoning beyond 3-4 steps. Performance drops from 85%+ on 2-step problems to under 30% on 5-step problems. Suggests architectural limitations rather than training data deficiency.
